articles.title: Single-Stock ETFs Beating the Market on Big "Mag 7" Wave
articles.summary: Single-stock ETFs have gained significant popularity, especially those linked to the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks. These ETFs provide leveraged exposure to individual companies like NVIDIA, Meta, Alphabet, Amazon, and Coinbase, often outperforming the broader market. While...
articles.content:
Single-Stock ETFs Beating the Market on Big "Mag 7" Wave

Single-stock ETFs have been gaining immense popularity over the past year amid the stock market surge and the big tech wave. Unlike traditional ETFs, which typically track a broad index or sector, single-stock ETFs provide exposure to the performance of one specific company by using derivatives. This allows investors to gain exposure to a particular stock without having to buy the stock directly.

Single-stock ETFs tap the gambling mindset that exists in markets. There are currently four dozen single-stock ETFs on the market with a combined $3.5 billion in assets, according to Morningstar data. Five firms, AXS, Direxion, YieldMax, GraniteShares and Innovator, provide all the single-stock ETFs currently available on the market.

We have highlighted five single-stock ETFs that have outperformed the market so far this year. The solid trend is likely to continue in the second half as well.

T-REX 2X Long NVIDIA Daily Target ETF (NVDX) – Up 449.5%

T-REX 2X Long NVIDIA Daily Target ETF seeks to magnify (200%) the daily performance of NVIDIA (NVDA). It has AUM of $653.6 million and an expense ratio of 1.05%. T-REX 2X Long NVIDIA Daily Target ETF trades in an average daily volume of 951,000 shares (read: ETFs to Tap as NVIDIA Becomes the Most Valuable Company).

GraniteShares 2x Long META Daily ETF (FBL) – Up 75.7%

GraniteShares 2x Long META Daily ETF tracks two times the performance of the stock of Meta Platforms (META). It has accumulated $129.2 million in its asset base and charges 1.15% in annual fees. GraniteShares 2x Long META Daily ETF trades in an average daily volume of 629,000 shares.

Direxion Daily GOOGL Bull 2X Shares (GGLL) – Up 56.4%

Direxion Daily GOOGL Bull 2X Shares tracks two times the performance of the Class A shares of Alphabet (GOOGL), charging 95 bps in annual fees. It trades in a volume of 187,000 shares a day on average and has accumulated assets worth $106.2 million.

Direxion Daily AMZN Bull 2X Shares ETF (AMZU) – Up 44.8%

Direxion Daily AMZN Bull 2X Shares ETF tracks two times the performance of the shares of Amazon (AMZN), charging 95 bps in annual fees. It trades in a volume of 443,000 shares a day on average and has accumulated assets worth $203.1 million.

GraniteShares 2X Long COIN Daily ETF (CONL) – Up 37.1%

GraniteShares 2x Long COIN Daily ETF seeks two times (200%) the daily percentage change of the common stock of Coinbase Global (COIN). It has accumulated $503.1 million in its asset base while trading in an average daily volume of 2.3 million shares. GraniteShares 1.5x Long COIN Daily ETF charges 1.15% in annual fees.

More Gains Ahead?

These best-performing ETFs are the leveraged plays on the so-called "Magnificent Seven" stocks. The "Magnificent Seven" is the biggest growth engine for the overall stock market. About 60% of the gains in the first half were driven by the “mega-cap” tech companies — NVIDIA, Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon, Meta Platforms and Apple (AAPL). NVIDIA alone accounted for 31% of the market’s first-half advance.

With the latest surge, NVIDIA, Apple and Microsoft are in the race to become the world’s most valuable company and hit a market capitalization of $4 trillion on surging enthusiasm over AI capabilities. This technology-driven momentum is expected to continue at least through the summer (read: Tech Sector Dominates First-Half Rally: 5 Best ETFs).

The expansion of AI applications holds the promise of ushering in fresh opportunities for growth in these stocks. According to a new report by Grand View Research, the global artificial intelligence market is expected to witness a CAGR (2024-2030) of 36.6% to reach $811.75 billion by 2030.

Further, these stocks have superior fundamentals with faster growth rates, higher profit margins, cleaner balance sheets and reasonable valuations.

For CONL, the prospect also looks bright. Though Bitcoin price dropped in recent months, investors’ interest in the mining sector surged following Core Scientific's (CORZ) deal with AI company CoreWeave in early June. Core Scientific signed a 200 megawatts (MW) artificial intelligence deal with the cloud computing firm, triggering a rally in the bitcoin mining sector (read: Is a $500K Bitcoin Rally Possible? Crypto ETFs to Consider).

Downsides

While single-stock ETFs offer a focused way to invest in a company, they come with significant risks due to their lack of diversification and exposure to the volatility of a single stock. They are typically more suited for experienced investors who understand and are willing to accept these risks. Here is the risk associated with these ETFs:

High Risk: If the specific company underperforms, investors could lose a substantial amount of money (read: Tap Tesla's Better-Than-Expected Q2 Deliveries With These ETFs).

Lack of Diversification: One of the key principles of risk management in investing is diversification. Single-stock ETFs go against this principle, as they are invested entirely in one company.

Market Volatility: A single-stock ETF is subject to the volatility of the individual stock, which can be influenced by company-specific news and events.

Management Fees: While typically lower than mutual funds, ETFs still come with management fees, which can eat into your investment returns over time, especially in a narrowly focused fund like a single-stock ETF.

Zacks Investment Research

articles.title: [424B2] JPMORGAN CHASE & CO Prospectus Supplement
articles.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC is offering $4.2 million in auto-callable contingent buffered equity notes linked to the S&P 500® Index, with a principal amount of $1,000 per note. These notes feature a 10.42% call premium if automatically called on the Review Date (July...
articles.content: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC is offering $4.2 million in auto-callable contingent buffered equity notes linked to the S&P 500® Index, with a principal amount of $1,000 per note. These notes feature a 10.42% call premium if automatically called on the Review Date (July 6, 2027) and a 20.84% Contingent Minimum Return if held to maturity (June 28, 2028) and the Ending Index Level is at or above the Index Strike Level. Investors face credit risk from JPMorgan Financial and JPMorgan Chase & Co., limited liquidity, and a potential loss of principal if the Ending Index Level falls below 80.00% of the Index Strike Level.

articles.title: TELUS stock holds steady on a thin tape. Investors watch cash flow and wireless demand.
articles.summary: TELUS (T, CA87971M1032) stock performance is being monitored by investors who are focusing on the company's cash flow and wireless demand, especially during a capital-intensive year. As a Canada-based communications company, TELUS relies on recurring revenue from its diverse o...
articles.content:
By Christina Vaughn, Background & Management desk. Reviewed on July 5, 2026 at 7:28 p.m. ET.

TELUS Corp. (CA87971M1032) remains a Canada-based communications company with a broad wireless, internet and business-services footprint, and it trades in Toronto on the TSX as TELUS Corp. For U.S. investors, the name is relevant mainly through its cross-border profile, recurring service revenue and exposure to telecom spending trends.

Business and capital needs

The company has long relied on subscription-based cash generation, but the sector also demands heavy network investment, especially in fiber and mobile infrastructure. That mix keeps free cash flow and leverage in sharp focus for TELUS as it balances growth spending with shareholder returns.

Its business model is built around consumer wireless plans, fixed internet and enterprise services, which gives the group a diversified revenue base compared with single-line hardware names. The service mix matters because it can soften the impact of hardware cycles and one-time sales swings.

What matters now

Investors in telecom names usually watch the same few variables first: subscriber additions, average revenue per user, capital expenditure and the pace of debt reduction. TELUS fits that pattern, and the stock tends to reflect whether management can defend margins while funding network upgrades.

A first. In this kind of setup, the market tends to reward discipline more than expansion for expansion's sake.

Service lines

Consumer wireless is the best-known part of the group, but TELUS also sells internet access, health technology and enterprise connectivity. Those categories matter because they give the company multiple ways to grow revenue without relying on a single product cycle.

Stock level

The shares were last quoted at a market level of CAD 19.20 as of July 5, 2026, 7:28 p.m. ET. The figure is a reference point for investors tracking how the market prices the company's cash flow profile and capital intensity.

This article was generated automatically and technically reviewed before publication. Market prices, analyst data and company information are provided without warranty and may change at short notice. articles.title: Amgen (AMGN) Could Be 45% Below Fair Value As Court Blocks Enbrel Price Cap
articles.summary: Amgen's stock is gaining momentum after a federal judge blocked Colorado's proposed 70% price cap on its key drug, Enbrel, removing a significant regulatory threat. While one fair value estimate suggests Amgen is modestly overvalued, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model indicate...
articles.content:
Amgen (AMGN) is back in focus after a federal judge blocked Colorado’s proposed 70% price cap on Enbrel, which removed an immediate regulatory threat to one of the company’s key revenue drivers.

The court decision has come on top of an already strong run in Amgen’s stock, with a 1-day share price return of 3.55% and a year to date share price return of 14.20%, while total shareholder return sits at 29.22% over one year and 87.63% over three years. This suggests momentum has been building despite recent regulatory noise around products like Tavneos and Corlanor.

If this regulatory win has you rethinking healthcare exposure, it may be worth widening your search to other drug developers and tools providers supported by AI by checking out 40 healthcare AI stocks

With Amgen now trading at $374.15, above one fair value estimate of $353.58 yet showing a 45.33% intrinsic discount on another model, you have to ask: is there still a buying opportunity here, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 6.2% Overvalued

With Amgen at $374.15 against a widely followed fair value estimate of about $352, the current price sits modestly above that narrative benchmark while still reflecting a business with meaningful earnings and revenue.

Advancements in personalized and targeted therapies, reflected in the robust late-stage pipeline (e.g., MariTide for obesity/type 2 diabetes, Repatha and olpasiran for cardiovascular, multiple bispecific T-cell engagers for oncology), position Amgen to launch high-margin, first-in-class products that drive both top-line growth and margin expansion in the coming years.

Read the complete narrative. Read the complete narrative.

The fair value hinges on measured revenue growth, higher profit margins, and a richer future earnings multiple. Want to see how those three ingredients come together and which assumptions really move the dial on Amgen's valuation story?

Result: Fair Value of $352.23 (OVERVALUED)

However, Amgen’s story can still be challenged if drug pricing pressures intensify or if biosimilar competition on key products eats into revenue and margins.

Another View: Amgen Through the DCF Lens

The earlier fair value of about $352 per share paints Amgen as modestly overvalued, but the SWS DCF model points in a different direction. On that measure, Amgen at $374.15 is trading below an estimated future cash flow value of $684.33, suggesting the cash flow story is far more generous than the price tag implies. Which version of fair value do you think better reflects the risks and rewards in front of you?

Simply Wall St performs a discounted cash flow (DCF) on every stock in the world every day (check out Amgen for example). We show the entire calculation in full. You can track the result in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted when this changes, or use our stock screener to discover 44 high quality undervalued stocks. If you save a screener we even alert you when new companies match - so you never miss a potential opportunity.

Next Steps

With sentiment on Amgen split between opportunity and caution, now is a good time to review the numbers, stress test the assumptions, and decide where you stand using the 3 key rewards and 1 important warning sign

Looking for more investment ideas beyond Amgen?

If the Amgen story has sharpened your focus, do not stop here. Use the Simply Wall St Screener to uncover other stocks that fit your approach.

  • Target potential value opportunities by checking companies that currently look mispriced using the 44 high quality undervalued stocks.
  • Strengthen your income approach by reviewing stocks that feature higher yields and consistent payments through the 7 dividend fortresses.
  • Prioritize resilience by focusing on companies with robust finances and sensible fundamentals via the solid balance sheet and fundamentals stocks screener (47 results).

articles.title: How Investors May Respond To Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) Deepening Its Role In Late‑Stage mRNA Manufacturing
articles.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has deepened its involvement in late-stage mRNA manufacturing through a collaboration with Arcturus Therapeutics for the ARCT-032 cystic fibrosis therapy. This agreement strengthens Thermo Fisher's position as an end-to-end development partner in compl...
articles.content:
• In late June and early July 2026, Arcturus Therapeutics announced a collaboration with Thermo Fisher Scientific, under which Thermo Fisher will supply integrated Phase 3 manufacturing, clinical research and potential commercial manufacturing services for ARCT-032, an investigational mRNA therapy for cystic fibrosis.
  • This agreement aligns Thermo Fisher even more closely with cutting-edge mRNA and rare disease workflows, expanding its role as an end-to-end development and manufacturing partner in complex biologics.
  • Next, we'll examine how Thermo Fisher’s deepening role in late-stage mRNA manufacturing through ARCT-032 could influence its investment narrative.

Capitalize on the AI infrastructure supercycle with our selection of the 52 best 'picks and shovels' of the AI gold rush converting record-breaking demand into massive cash flow.

Thermo Fisher Scientific Investment Narrative Recap

To own Thermo Fisher, you generally need to believe in its role as a diversified picks-and-shovels provider to pharma, biotech and diagnostics, with resilient demand for its tools, consumables and services even when individual end markets are choppy. The Arcturus ARCT-032 collaboration reinforces Thermo Fisher’s end-to-end presence in complex biologics, but it does not materially change the near term focus on lab spending trends and China exposure as key catalysts and risks.

Among recent announcements, the AustinPx KinetiSol agreement is especially relevant, because it broadens Thermo Fisher’s advanced oral drug manufacturing toolkit in a way that echoes the ARCT-032 mRNA deal: both deepen its role as a development-to-commercialization partner. Together, these moves sit alongside upcoming Q2 2026 earnings as important reference points for how well Thermo Fisher is converting its expanded capabilities into growth while managing margin and funding headwinds.

Yet while these partnerships look promising, investors should also be aware that persistent weakness in China and tariff-related margin pressure could...

Thermo Fisher Scientific's narrative projects $54.1 billion revenue and $9.5 billion earnings by 2029. This requires 6.2% yearly revenue growth and about a $2.7 billion earnings increase from $6.8 billion today.

Uncover how Thermo Fisher Scientific's forecasts yield a $611.56 fair value, a 17% upside to its current price.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Compared with consensus, the most optimistic analysts already expected Thermo Fisher to reach about US$56.0 billion in revenue and US$10.2 billion in earnings by 2029, so this new ARCT-032 partnership could either support that upbeat view or highlight how sensitive those forecasts are to trade and China risks if conditions shift.

Explore 6 other fair value estimates on Thermo Fisher Scientific - why the stock might be worth 6% less than the current price!

Reach Your Own Conclusion

Disagree with existing narratives? Extraordinary investment returns rarely come from following the herd, so go with your instincts.

  • A great starting point for your Thermo Fisher Scientific research is our analysis highlighting 3 key rewards and 1 important warning sign that could impact your investment decision.
  • Our free Thermo Fisher Scientific research report provides a comprehensive fundamental analysis summarized in a single visual - the Snowflake - making it easy to evaluate Thermo Fisher Scientific's overall financial health at a glance.

articles.title: Rosen Law Firm Urges Insulet Corporation (NASDAQ: PODD) Stockholders with Large Losses to Contact the Firm for Information About Their Rights
articles.summary: The Rosen Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit against Insulet Corporation (NASDAQ: PODD) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between February 21, 2025, and May 26, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Insulet made false or misleading statements regarding defect...
articles.content:
Rosen Law Firm, a global investor rights law firm, announces a class action lawsuit on behalf of purchasers and acquirers of Insulet Corporation (NASDAQ: PODD) securities between February 21, 2025 and May 26, 2026, inclusive (the “Class Period”). Insulet develops, manufactures, and sells insulin delivery systems.

For more information, submit a form, email attorney Phillip Kim, or give us a call at 866-767-3653.

The Allegations: Rosen Law Firm is Investigating the Allegations that Insulet Corporation (NASDAQ: PODD) Misled Investors Regarding its Business Operations.

According to the lawsuit, throughout the Class Period, defendants made false and/or misleading statements and/or failed to disclose that: (1) Insulet's manufacturing controls and procedures were defective; (2) the foregoing created a foreseeable heightened risk that one or more Insulet products would be found to be in violation of applicable safety regulations and/or pose a risk of injury; and (3) as a result, defendants' public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times. When the true details entered the market, the lawsuit claims that investors suffered damages.

What Now: You may be eligible to participate in the class action against Insulet. Shareholders who want to serve as lead plaintiff for the class must file their motions with the court by August 31, 2026. A lead plaintiff is a representative party who acts on behalf of other class members in directing the litigation. You do not have to participate in the case to be eligible for a recovery. If you choose to take no action, you can remain an absent class member. For more information, click here.

All representation is on a contingency fee basis. Shareholders pay no fees or expenses.

About Rosen Law Firm: Some law firms issuing releases about this matter do not actually litigate securities class actions. Rosen Law Firm does. Rosen Law Firm is a recognized leader in shareholder rights litigation, dedicated to helping shareholders recover losses, improving corporate governance structures, and holding company executives accountable for their wrongdoing. Since its inception, Rosen Law Firm has obtained over $1 billion for shareholders.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-rosen-law-firm, on Twitter: https://twitter.com/rosen_firm or on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/rosenlawfirm/.

articles.title: 3M balances transformation and litigation costs as investors watch the next phase
articles.summary: 3M Company is undergoing significant restructuring and portfolio streamlining while simultaneously handling substantial litigation settlements. The company aims to stabilize earnings and cash flow through operational efficiency and focused capital allocation. Investors are clo...
articles.content:
By Thomas Clarke, Operations & Strategy desk. Reviewed on July 5, 2026 at 7:02 p.m. ET.

3M Company (ISIN US88579Y1010) is a diversified industrial and technology group that has been reshaping its portfolio and cost base while dealing with sizable litigation and settlement obligations. The company remains a well-known US-based manufacturer with broad exposure to industrial, consumer, healthcare and safety markets. For investors, the balance between transformation spending and underlying cash generation is a central theme.

Restructuring and portfolio streamlining

In recent years, 3M has pursued restructuring programs aimed at simplifying its structure and reducing costs. These initiatives have included workforce reductions, consolidation of manufacturing and administrative sites, and efforts to streamline the supply chain and overhead. The goal has been to improve operational efficiency, adapt to changing demand patterns and support margins in a more volatile macroeconomic environment.

The company has also worked on portfolio streamlining, focusing more closely on businesses with stronger growth profiles or technology advantages. That has involved divesting or winding down smaller or less strategic units and concentrating resources on segments where its materials science, adhesives, filtration, abrasives and healthcare technologies provide competitive differentiation. Such portfolio moves are designed to sharpen the group’s focus and potentially reduce earnings volatility over time.

Litigation, settlements and cash flow

Alongside restructuring, 3M has faced significant litigation exposure, including large settlement agreements related to product and environmental matters. These obligations have required substantial cash outflows and provisions, influencing free cash flow and leverage metrics. Analysts often highlight that managing these payments, while maintaining investment in core operations and innovation, is one of the company’s key financial challenges.

In response, 3M has emphasized disciplined capital allocation, working to balance shareholder returns, debt management and funding for restructuring and legal commitments. The company’s diversified revenue base across industrial, safety, healthcare and consumer categories offers resilience, but cash and balance-sheet flexibility remain under close market scrutiny as settlement timelines and amounts play out.

Representative product and innovation approach

3M is known for applying materials science and engineering to practical applications, and its brand portfolio spans thousands of products used in everyday life and industrial settings. A representative example is its family of adhesive and bonding solutions, which are used across automotive manufacturing, electronics assembly, construction, packaging and consumer applications. These products illustrate how the company combines chemistry, process know-how and application support to help customers improve durability, efficiency or ease of use.

Stock listing and trading context

3M Company stock is listed in the United States, where it is widely followed by domestic and international investors as part of the large-cap industrial segment. The shares are considered a bellwether for certain manufacturing, safety and consumer demand trends because the company’s operations touch many end markets. For investors, monitored metrics typically include earnings development, free cash flow generation, progress on restructuring and the evolving impact of litigation-related cash commitments.

This article was generated automatically and technically reviewed before publication. Market prices, analyst data and company information are provided without warranty and may change at short notice. articles.title: Tsakos Energy Navigation (TEN) Adds Another LNG Carrier As Fair Value Debate Builds
articles.summary: Tsakos Energy Navigation (TEN) has ordered a second LNG carrier, increasing its newbuilding program to 20 vessels, which has contributed to strong stock performance this year. While many analysts consider the stock undervalued at $46, Simply Wall St's DCF model suggests a fair...
articles.content:
Tsakos Energy Navigation (TEN) has ordered a second LNG carrier from Hyundai Heavy Industries, bringing its newbuilding program to 20 vessels and adding to secured revenue tied to long term charter commitments.

Tsakos Energy Navigation shares are trading at $37.37 after a 1-day share price return of 5.65%. This extends a strong year to date share price return of 70.72%, while the 1-year total shareholder return of 95.26% reflects sizeable gains over a longer horizon, despite a recent 90-day share price decline of 8.20%.

With Tsakos Energy Navigation stock up 70.72% year to date and trading at a discount to the average analyst price target, you have to ask: Is the recent LNG push still underappreciated, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 18.8% Undervalued

Against the last close of $37.37, the most followed narrative pins Tsakos Energy Navigation's fair value at $46, which naturally raises questions about what is built into that gap.

The company's significant investment in fleet modernization with a focus on eco-friendly, dual-fuel, and high-specification vessels positions it to secure higher time charter rates from energy majors, control operating expenses, and meet upcoming environmental regulations, all of which should improve net margins and earnings resilience. Growing long-term contracted revenue backlog (between $3.7 and $4.0 billion, representing more than $120 per share) with blue-chip oil majors amid global energy security concerns provides strong revenue visibility and reduces earnings volatility, supporting prospective dividend growth and underpinning a higher intrinsic valuation.

Want to see what sits behind that $46 fair value for Tsakos Energy Navigation? The narrative leans on specific revenue paths, margin compression, and a future earnings multiple that looks very different to today. Curious which assumptions have to hold for that outcome to make sense.

However, the Tsakos Energy Navigation story still faces pressure if long term oil transport demand softens or if high net debt and refinancing needs start to have a significant impact.

Another View: Tsakos Energy Navigation Through a Cash Flow Lens

The most followed Tsakos Energy Navigation narrative leans on a $46 fair value, yet the SWS DCF model paints a very different picture, placing future cash flows at $14.12 per share. This suggests the stock is trading above that estimate. For you, the question is which set of assumptions feels more realistic.

Simply Wall St performs a discounted cash flow (DCF) on every stock in the world every day (check out Tsakos Energy Navigation for example). We show the entire calculation in full. You can track the result in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted when this changes, or use our stock screener to discover 44 high quality undervalued stocks. If you save a screener we even alert you when new companies match - so you never miss a potential opportunity.

Next Steps

With mixed signals around Tsakos Energy Navigation, investors may wish to move quickly to evaluate the potential upside alongside the concerns using the detailed breakdown of 2 key rewards and 3 important warning signs

articles.title: Is Super Group (SGHC) Cheap On Buyback Approval And Strong Earnings Expectations?
articles.summary: Super Group (SGHC) is gaining attention due to a recently approved share repurchase authorization and positive earnings expectations. While analysts see an 19.9% undervaluation with a fair value of $18.13, its current P/E ratio of 30.1x is higher than the industry average, sug...
articles.content:
Super Group (SGHC) (NYSE:SGHC) is back in focus after shareholders approved a sizable share repurchase authorization, coinciding with earnings expectations and analyst sentiment around revenue and net income growth forecasts.

At a share price of $14.51, Super Group (SGHC) has shown strong momentum, with a 30 day share price return of 12.13% and a 90 day share price return of 35.48%. The 3 year total shareholder return is very large, reflecting how recent earnings expectations and the new buyback authorization are feeding into both short and longer term sentiment.

If Super Group’s recent run has your attention, it can be useful to compare it with other companies benefiting from powerful long term themes such as artificial intelligence infrastructure, using the 52 AI infrastructure stocks

With Super Group (SGHC) trading at $14.51 and sitting at a sizeable intrinsic discount alongside upbeat earnings expectations, the key question now is simple: is this a genuine value opportunity or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 19.9% Undervalued

At $14.51, the most widely followed narrative on Super Group (SGHC) points to a fair value of $18.13, anchored on detailed earnings and revenue assumptions built using an 8.42% discount rate.

The analysts have a consensus price target of $18.12 for Super Group (SGHC) based on their expectations of its future earnings growth, profit margins and other risk factors.

However, there is a degree of disagreement amongst analysts, with the more bullish reporting a price target of $22.0, and the most bearish reporting a price target of just $16.0.

Curious what sits underneath that $18.13 fair value for Super Group? The narrative leans heavily on compounding revenue, rising margins and a future earnings multiple that needs careful reading.

Result: Fair Value of $18.13 (UNDERVALUED)

However, the Super Group (SGHC) narrative can be challenged if regulatory pressure tightens further or if heavy technology spending does not translate into stronger profitability.

Another View: What Super Group (SGHC)’s P/E Ratio Is Telling You

The earlier fair value of $18.13 suggests Super Group (SGHC) is 19.9% undervalued, but the current P/E of 30.1x tells a different story. It is higher than the US Hospitality industry at 23.8x and slightly above a fair ratio of 28.2x, pointing to a valuation that already bakes in optimistic expectations.

Compared with peers on 38x, the stock does not screen as the most expensive option, yet it is not a clear bargain either. For investors, the tension is simple: are you comfortable paying a premium P/E today in the hope that earnings forecasts and narrative assumptions hold up?

Next Steps

If the mixed signals around Super Group (SGHC) have you torn, now is the time to examine the details, weigh the concerns and opportunities, and judge whether the 4 key rewards and 2 important warning signs matches your view of the stock.

Looking for more investment ideas beyond Super Group (SGHC)?

Do not stop with one stock. Broaden your radar and compare Super Group (SGHC) with other opportunities that might better fit your goals and risk comfort.

articles.title: Turkcell stock holds focus on Istanbul. Investor relations remains the main anchor.
articles.summary: This article focuses on Turkcell, a major Turkish telecom operator, and its sustained focus on its Istanbul stock listing and investor relations. It details the company's business model, encompassing mobile, broadband, and digital services, and highlights the factors investors...
articles.content:
By Christina Vaughn, Background & Management desk. Reviewed on July 5, 2026 at 5:52 p.m. ET.

Turkcell (ISIN TRATCELL91M1) remains a closely watched Turkish telecom name with a clear investor relations presence and a listed market profile in Istanbul. Its business spans mobile, broadband and digital services, which keeps the company tied to both consumer demand and network investment cycles.

Company profile

Turkcell Iletisim Hizmetleri A.S. operates as one of Turkey's largest communications groups, serving subscribers across mobile voice, data and fixed services. For US readers, the stock is best understood through its home-market listing and its role as a regional telecom operator rather than through a New York trading venue.

What matters now

With no fresh source set to anchor a narrower catalyst, the practical focus stays on operating trends, subscriber quality and capital spending. Telecom investors typically watch revenue mix, data usage and network investments because those factors shape both growth and margin pressure.

Services and revenue mix

Turkcell's core business is mobile connectivity, but the company also sells fixed broadband and digital offerings that extend its reach beyond basic voice service. That mix matters because it gives the group several ways to offset slower growth in any single line.

Stock and venue

Turkcell trades in Istanbul, and its market read-through is driven by Turkey's telecom and macro backdrop more than by US index membership. As of July 5, 2026, no live price was supplied in the available source set, so the article keeps to the verified company profile and trading venue only.

articles.title: Tsakos Energy Navigation (TEN) Adds Another LNG Carrier As Fair Value Debate Builds
articles.summary: Tsakos Energy Navigation (TEN) has ordered a second LNG carrier, expanding its newbuilding program to 20 vessels, which adds to its secured revenue through long-term charter commitments. The company's stock has seen significant gains year-to-date and over the past year, tradin...
articles.content: Tsakos Energy Navigation (TEN) has ordered a second LNG carrier, expanding its newbuilding program to 20 vessels, which adds to its secured revenue through long-term charter commitments. The company's stock has seen significant gains year-to-date and over the past year, trading at a discount to the average analyst price target. While a narrative suggests TEN is 18.8% undervalued with a fair value of $46 due to fleet modernization and growing contracted revenue, another model using discounted cash flow places its future cash flows at $14.12 per share, indicating the stock might be trading above that estimate.

articles.title: Spotify Stock is Down 38% From All-Time High, But One Analyst Sees a 40% Upside
articles.summary: Spotify's stock has fallen 38% from its all-time high, but a Bank of America analyst predicts a strong comeback, seeing a 40% upside due to its product roadmap, financial targets, and potential for growth from price increases and new services. Other analysts from Cantor Fitzge...
articles.content:
Bank of America is Bullish on Spotify Stock

Spotify stock has been in a downward trend, a situation that worsened a few months ago when it published its quarterly earnings. Its report showed that its revenue rose by 8% to $5.3 billion, while its monthly active users soared 12% to 761 million.

Despite its company publishing strong numbers, the stock retreated because its guidance was a bit soft, with management guiding to 6 million premium subscriber additions in the second quarter. Analysts were expecting the real premium subscribers to be 300.4 million.

In a note, a Bank of America analyst predicted that the company was well positioned to grow this year. Jessica Reif Ehrlich, the analyst, added that Spotify has:

"laid out an impressive compelling product roadmap and attractive financial targets, and now the focus will turn to execution. We now see even greater clarity for continued profit and [free cash flow] growth including price increases, new tiers and further penetration of incremental services including podcasting, audiobooks and fitness."

Other top analysts tracking the company believe that its stock has more upside ahead. Cantor Fitzgerald boosted the target to $520 from the previous $430.

Doug Anmuth, a JPMorgan analyst, hiked his target from $600 to $650, while Wells Fargo hiked to $600 from $580. As a result, according to Benzinga, the consensus target for the SPOT stock is $678, also up by 40% from the current level.

Spotify Dominates the Music Streaming Industry

Most analysts cite Spotify’s market share in the music streaming industry and its role in the booming podcasting sector. It has beaten other popular brands in the streaming industry, including those by companies like Apple, Amazon, and Google.

Analysts expect that its annual revenue will grow by 13.25% this year to $22.37 billion, followed by 14.2% next year to $25 billion. The company has also been expanding its margins. Its recent earnings showed that its net income margin rose to 15.46%.

Still, the company has also come under pressure following its launch of AI-generated remixes and song covers. It introduced the feature in a partnership with Universal Music Group, the biggest music label company in the world.

The next key catalyst for Spotify stock will be its earnings, which will come out on August 4. Analysts expect its revenue to rise by 14% in the second quarter.

Image: Shutterstock

articles.title: Micron (MU) Stock Faces Michael Burry Short As Legal Risks Linger
articles.summary: Michael Burry has opened a short position against Micron Technology (MU) despite the company reporting record earnings driven by AI memory demand. This move adds to the stock's volatility, which is already influenced by an ongoing class-action lawsuit alleging price fixing and...
articles.content:
Make better investment decisions with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.

  • Michael Burry has disclosed a short position against Micron Technology (NasdaqGS:MU), targeting one of the market's closely watched AI hardware stocks.

  • The move comes as Micron reports record earnings supported by AI-related memory demand and long-term customer agreements.

  • Investor attention is also on an active class-action lawsuit over alleged price fixing and recent insider share sales, which have added to share price volatility.

Micron Technology sits at the center of the AI hardware story, with its memory products tied directly to high-performance computing demand. The stock last closed at $975.56 and is up 12.9% over the past month and 209.3% year to date. Over the past three years, the share price has increased more than 15x, making Micron one of the stand-out performers in the sector.

Burry's short adds a fresh challenge to an already polarizing stock and could influence how investors think about risk around NasdaqGS:MU. With legal questions, insider activity, and strong operational results all in play, the next phase for Micron is likely to be shaped by sentiment and headlines as well as by quarterly numbers.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Micron Technology by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Micron Technology.

Quick Assessment

  • ❌ Price vs Analyst Target: Micron Technology trades at US$975.56, around 34% below the consensus target of US$1,486, with a wide analyst range between US$361 and US$2,200.

  • ❌ Simply Wall St Valuation: Shares are flagged as overvalued, trading about 71.4% above the estimated fair value.

  • ✅ Recent Momentum: The stock is up 12.9% over the last 30 days, indicating strong short term momentum despite the new short position.

There's only one way to know the right time to buy, sell or hold Micron Technology. Head to Simply Wall St's company report for the latest analysis of Micron Technology's Fair Value.

Key Considerations

  • 📊 Burry's short against Micron Technology challenges a strong earnings story and may intensify scrutiny on how much optimism is already in the price.

  • 📊 Watch the P/E of 21.8x versus the Semiconductor industry average of 65.7x, analyst target revisions, and any updates on long term customer agreements.

  • ⚠️ The combination of an active class action, significant insider selling, and a valuation flagged as overvalued creates headline and sentiment risk if expectations slip.

articles.title: More than 150,000 people without power in Southeast Michigan over a day after storms roll through area
articles.summary: Over 150,000 residents in Southeast Michigan remain without power on Sunday afternoon, more than a day after severe storms hit the area. DTE Energy expects to restore service to 85-90% of affected customers by the end of Sunday, with some potentially waiting until early next w...
articles.content:
More than 130,000 Southeast Michigan residents are without power Sunday afternoon, more than a day after severe storms swept through the area, according to DTE Energy.

As of 4:30 p.m., 130,434 people are without service, according to the energy company's outage map said. According to PowerOutage.US, over 83,000 individuals in Wayne County, 20,000-plus in Oakland County, 10,000-plus in Monroe County and 9,000-plus in Macomb County do not have power.

Brian Calka, senior vice president of distribution operations with DTE Energy, said in a news conference Sunday morning that the company expects between 85% and 90% of customers impacted by Friday's storms to have service by the end of the day, with the rest getting power back "early this week."

"There are customers who will, most likely, be out of power until Tuesday. We are communicating with those customers," Calka said. "We are reaching out to them to let them know what that expectation looks like, but please rest assured we are doing everything in our power to move that restoration timeline into tomorrow, if at all possible."

According to company officials, crews restored power to over 200,000 people within 24 hours of the severe weather.

Calka said that Friday's storms were one of the most impactful in regard to damage to the company's system that he's seen in his 20 years in the energy industry. Around 2,000 power lines were down after the severe weather, with a large part of the damage happening in Monroe County.

"We have many cases where we have outages with eight to 10 to 12 broken poles and in order to restore all the customers who are impacted by that outage, every one of those poles has to be replaced," he said. "To replace a pole, it's anywhere from three to four to five hours, depending on the location of that pole."

Mayor Abdullah Hammoud of Dearborn, Michigan, said there were at least , along with five water main breaks in the city on Saturday morning.

There were roughly 60 downed power lines in Warren, Michigan, on Saturday afternoon, according to Mayor Lori Stone.

The Detroit Zoo was closed on Saturday due to power outages at the facility, officials said. It reopened on Sunday morning.
